stories.title: EU to ‘convey concerns’ to US about Vance’s Hungary intervention – as it happened
stories.summary: US vice-president has praised Orbán and criticised EU and UK energy policies in speech at private school in Budapest Oh, you can see where this is going to go. In his second question, the moderator tries to bait JD Vance into criticising Ukraine, as the chair asks about what he says are “Ukrainian intelligence services attempting to influence” elections in the US or Hungary. “I’ve also been told that the vice-president of the United States coming and saying that Viktor Orbán is doing a good job

stories.title: What Péter Magyar learned from the last guy to lose to Viktor Orbán
stories.summary: HÓDMEZŐVÁSÁRHELY, Hungary — When opposition leader Péter Márki-Zay failed to topple Prime Minister Viktor Orbán in the country’s 2022 election, many Hungarians said the result showed only an insider could win, using the hardball tactics of the ruling Fidesz party. Péter Magyar, who leads the polls ahead of Sunday’s election , is exactly such a candidate — a Fidesz veteran who was married to Orbán’s former justice minister and understands how to play the prime minister at his own game

stories.title: Euroskeptic think tanks on the rise as Brussels slashes funding for pro-EU groups
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― The European Commission cut millions of euros of funding from established pro-EU think tanks ― just as more Euroskeptic groups have been gaining ground. European think tanks say they’re having to scale back their activities or lay off staff since the Commission scrapped a large chunk of their funding last year; the funding shortfall amounts to €7.8 million, according to French news outlet L’Informé . The funding cut came months after conservative organizations, including Hungary&#8217

stories.title: JD Vance claims US is not interfering in Hungary election
stories.summary: US vice-president says on visit to Budapest ‘we had to show’ support for Viktor Orbán, as opposition leads polls Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? JD Vance has pushed back against claims that the US is interfering in Hungarian politics, describing the accusations as “darkly ironic”, as a set of polls suggested the opposition Tisza party could win a supermajority in the forthcoming elections. After spending his first day in Budapest excoriating the EU and accusing it o

stories.title: JD Vance’s endorsement of Orbán - podcast
stories.summary: Flora Garamvolgyi on JD Vance’s visit to Hungary in the run-up to the country’s elections on Sunday “Hungary has been a model for the Trump presidency for a while now,” the Guardian journalist Flora Garamvolgyi tells Helen Pidd. “And US Republicans looked at Hungary for these past years as a model to follow. “[Viktor] Orbán is currently on his fourth consecutive term. And the fact that he has been so successful and he had similar narrative, similar ideologies to US Republicans in terms of immigr

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: We'll keep quiet until after the Hungarian elections – Vučić on explosives found near gas pipeline
stories.summary: “Anyone who claims that the Serbian army wants to interfere in the Hungarian elections should be ashamed of themselves,” the Serbian president said. Until they know for certain what happened, they will not disclose any details about the explosives found near the Turkish Stream pipeline.

stories.title: Carnival celebrations at a Hungarian retirement home: János Bődey’s best photograph
stories.summary: ‘I asked them the secret to a happy life. “Good genes,” replied Magdolna, who was 87. “Avoid borrowing money,” added Irén, 86’ When I saw these two elderly ladies dressed as bride and groom in a retirement home in Páty, near Budapest, my first thought was to wonder why they had chosen those particular costumes. I took their picture in the home’s lounge and afterwards we had a long conversation. I asked them about their lives, what they believed was the secret to a long life, and how to preserve

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Orbán titkára üzent Wáberer Györgynek: Mélységes mély a csalódás bennem irányodban
stories.summary: Nagy János államtitkár árulónak nevezte a volt kormánybiztost.

stories.title: Washington Post: Szijjártó segítséget ajánlott Iránnak a Hezbollah csipogóinak felrobbantása után
stories.summary: Mindig állok a szolgálatára – mondta a magyar külügyminiszter iráni kollégájának 2024 őszén az amerikai lap által megszerzett kormányzati leirat szerint.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Nagykanizsán: Bármennyire is jól nézünk ki, nyomjuk meg – 2026 élőben
stories.summary: Hann Endréék biztos kétharmadot mértek a Tiszának, de Magyar Péter szerint senki ne hagyja magát elaltatni, nem közvélemény-kutatást kell nyerni. Magyar Péter országjárása Nagykanizsára, Orbán Viktoré Sopronba érkezett, a Telex stúdiójában pedig Cseke Balázzsal és Mizsur Andrással, a Telex újságíróival foglaltuk össze az elmúlt 24 órát.

stories.summary: A külügyminiszter a Washington Post cikkére azt reagálta, hogy külföldi titkosszolgálatok befolyásolni akarják a magyar választásokat.

stories.title: Kreml szóvivője: Putyin és Orbán beszélgetésének leirata a magyar miniszterelnök mellett szóló érv
stories.summary: Dmitrij Peszkov szerint a kiszivárgott beszélgetés alapján Orbán Viktor pragmatikus és hatékony politikus

stories.title: Orbán Sopronban: Nekünk vasárnap este hárommillió szavazatra van szükségünk, és akkor lesz nemzeti egység
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerdán Sopronban folytatta a kampányát, ahol néhány tüntető is várta. Orbán szerint J. D. Vance látogatása bizonyítja, hogy nem lehet a kormányt oroszbarátsággal gyanúsítani.

stories.title: Balogh Nikolett főnővér lett 2026-ban a hétköznapok roma hőse
stories.summary: Az idei Aranypánt-díj jelöltjeire több mint hatezer szavazat érkezett.

stories.title: A Tisza-szlogent skandáló, megvert fiatalok melletti kiállásból káosz és vádaskodás lett Sepsiszentgyörgyön
stories.summary: Politikai csatatérré vált az erőszakellenes flashmob az OSK-s edzők verekedős botránya után. A Tisza Párt sepsiszentgyörgyi származású tagja Bánk bán-áriával válaszolt a hazaárulózásra.

stories.title: Lavrov to Szijjártó: Ok, Peter, if you can send me the document, I would appreciate this
stories.summary: Audio recordings reveal how the Hungarian and Russian foreign ministers coordinated ahead of Orbán’s trip to Moscow in July 2024, and what kind of assistance Szijjártó promised Lavrov.

stories.title: Ritkábban ütközik a tram-train a sárgítás óta
stories.summary: A vasúttársaság statisztikái szerint észrevehetőbbek lettek a tram-trainek a sárga matricákkal, de folyamatosan vizsgálják, hogy mivel lehet továbbfejleszteni a közlekedésbiztonságot.

stories.title: Idegenbeli döntetlennel jutott a Pick Szeged a kézi-BL negyeddöntőjébe
stories.summary: Az 53. percben 26-30 volt, kiesésre állt a magyar csapat, de Bodó és Mikler a legjobbkor villant a hajrában.

stories.title: Közel 900 magyar fiatal nyerhet ingyen európai utazást az EU pályázatán
stories.summary: Szerdától lehet jelentkezni a DiscoverEU pályázatra, amivel 41 ezer európai fiatal utazgathat ingyen egy hónapig.

stories.title: A PSG lefocizta a Liverpoolt a BL-ben, az emberelőnybe kerülő Atlético Barcelonában győzött
stories.summary: A Bajnokok Ligája szerdai negyeddöntőiben kétgólos győzelmek születtek. A PSG sikere nem meglepő az erőtlen, kaput sem találó Liverpool ellen, az Atlético barcelonai győzelme annál inkább.

stories.title: Trump bejelentette a tűzszünetet, a világ fellélegzett, aztán fél nappal később minden bizonytalan lett
stories.summary: Az olaj ára csökkenni kezdett, a részvénypiacok pedig emelkedtek. Szerda estére azonban komoly ellentmondások látszanak az USA és Irán nyilvános kommunikációjában a tűzszünet feltételeiről és a tárgyalások állásáról. Izrael Libanonban tovább támadja a Hezbollahot. Trump szerint ez nem része a tűzszünetnek, Iránból viszont válaszlépésekkel fenyegettek.

stories.title: Magyar Péter nagykanizsai gyűlésén is megjelentek orosz tudósítók, a tömeg pedig azt skandálta: „ruszkik, haza”
stories.summary: Szerdán Kaposvárra és Nagykanizsára is ellátogatott a Tisza Párt elnöke. Míg Magyar Péter hat, addig Orbán Viktor egy helyszínen, Sopronban kampányolt. Magyarra elkötelezett közönség ment ki, de van, aki szerint akkor sem váltja meg az országot, ha lesz pénz.

stories.title: Billions in state funds secretly channeled to Viktor Orbán’s favourite company before defense acquisitions
stories.summary: According to documents obtained by Direkt36, state funds were secretly directed to a company called 4iG. This happened at the very time when the government decided to sell state-owned defense companies to this firm.

stories.title: Moscow threatens Baltic states, claiming they help Ukraine strike Russia
stories.summary: The Russian foreign ministry issued veiled threats to Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania, accusing them of assisting Ukraine in Kyiv’s campaign against Russia’s oil terminals.

stories.summary: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. stories.title: Vance’s whirlwind visit may not help Orbán to the election victory he craves
stories.summary: As the US vice-president wades into a heated campaign, Hungary’s leader faces the real possibility of defeat Even before the plane carrying JD and Usha Vance had landed in Budapest, the Hungarian government had hailed their two-day visit as a new golden age in the relationship between Washington and Budapest. What came next was a whirlwind of politics in which the US vice-president waded directly into the country’s heated election campaign, just days before Hungarians cast their ballots. Continu

stories.title: Kollégánk, Vorák Anita is elnyerte a MÚOSZ sajtódíját az Orbán eseményeire szervezett skandálókról szóló anyagáért
stories.summary: A MÚOSZ négy anyagot is díjazott márciusban, mivel a szervezet szerint mindegyik megérdemli az elismerést.

stories.title: Ha Orbán veszít, oda a támogatások – ezzel buzdítják szavazásra a csángókat egy helyi szerint
stories.summary: A moldvai csángók lakta Gajcsánáról fordult egy civil szervezethez egy választó. Levelében részletesen leírja, hogyan bombázzák politikai üzenetekkel a helyieket, hogy leadják a levélszavazataikat. A csángószövetség elnöke nem is érti, miről beszél.

stories.title: A Telexnél folytatja Tamás Bence Gáspár
stories.summary: A magyar internetes videós újságírás egyik megteremtője saját anyagok készítése és szerkesztése mellett a lap kreatív folyamataiban is részt vesz majd.

stories.title: Vučić a robbanószerekről: Hallgatunk, amíg a magyarországi választások le nem zárulnak
stories.summary: Szégyellje magát, aki azt állítja, hogy a szerb hadsereg be akar avatkozni a magyar választásokba – mondta a szerb elnök. Amíg nem tudják biztosan, mi történt, nem osztanak meg részleteket a Török Áramlatnál talált robbanószerről.

stories.title: Lavrov Szijjártónak: Oké, Péter, ha elküldené nekem a dokumentumot, nagyon hálás lennék érte
stories.summary: Hangfelvételekből kiderült, hogy hogyan egyeztetett a magyar és az orosz külügyminiszter Orbán moszkvai útja előtt, és hogy milyen segítséget ígért Szijjártó Lavrovnak.

stories.title: Szijjártó az újabb hangfelvételekről: Szokatlanul durva és nyílt titkosszolgálati beavatkozás
stories.summary: A magyar miniszter idézőjeles leleplezésnek nevezte a két újabb felvételt, amiken Szergej Lavrov orosz külügyminiszterrel egyeztet.

stories.title: Orbán: Nem ismerem név szerint azokat, akik Dubajban szoktak lenni, nem akarok róluk rosszat mondani
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a Blikknek adott interjújában beszélt az MNB-ügyről és Matolcsyról is.

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Orbán offered help to Putin 'in any way that I can,' reports say
stories.summary: Orbán offered his support to Putin, likening his assistance to that of a mouse helping a lion in a well-known Hungarian fable, according to a phone conversation transcript obtained by news agency Bloomberg.

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says
stories.summary: Australian federal police say they are working with tiny nation to respond to threat of online scam centres Timor-Leste is vulnerable to “infiltration by foreign organized crime”, the country’s president, José Ramos-Horta, has warned. His comments come as Australian federal police confirmed to the Guardian the force is providing support to local law enforcement in Timor-Leste, including a December 2025 visit from the agency’s digital forensic and cyber experts. Continue reading...

stories.title: Russian GRU exploited vulnerable routers worldwide to steal sensitive information, probe reveals
stories.summary: An international investigation revealed how Russia’s GRU cyber unit, known as "Fancy Bear," managed to steal sensitive information from governments and the military using badly protected routers.

stories.title: Trump threatens 50 percent tariffs on Iran arms supplies. His legal path is murky.
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday threatened 50 percent tariffs on any country that supplies military weapons to Iran, though it’s not clear he has the legal authority to do so. “A Country supplying Military Weapons to Iran will be immediately tariffed, on any and all goods sold to the United States of America, 50%, effective immediately. There will be no exclusions or exemptions!” Trump  wrote in a post on Truth Social . While the president previously wielded tariffs to pressure other co

stories.title: Israel greets Iran ceasefire with more unease than relief
stories.summary: TEL AVIV, Israel — Israel greeted the Iran ceasefire with more unease than relief, as overnight missile fire, sharp political backlash and deep skepticism about Tehran’s intentions reinforced a broad sense that the conflict is paused, but far from resolved. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu faced swift criticism from political opponents on the left and right, who slammed the Israeli premier for failing to eliminate the core threats posed by Iran’s nuclear program, missile arsenal and regional pr

stories.title: IDF conduct in Lebanon plunges Israel-Europe relations to lower depths
stories.summary: Spain and Italy have demanded explanations from Israel after the country’s soldiers in Lebanon detained a Spanish United Nations peacekeeper and fired warning shots at an Italian convoy. The incidents mark another low point in Israel’s relations with Europe, already severely damaged by the war in Gaza and now further strained by the country’s ongoing military campaign in Lebanon. Italian Defense Minister Guido Crosetto stated on Wednesday that Italy “demands full respect for th

stories.title: Trump’s ceasefire already under strain
stories.summary: The Pakistani leader at the center of U.S.-Iran peace talks denounced “violations” that threaten to derail the new ceasefire, as the White House downplayed reports Wednesday that Tehran is again closing the Strait of Hormuz. “Violations of ceasefire have been reported at few places across the conflict zone which undermine the spirit of peace process,” Prime Minister Shehbaz  Sharif wrote in a post on X Wednesday . “I earnestly and sincerely urge all parties to exercise restraint and respect

stories.title: Starmer says UK wants to help with opening of Hormuz strait on Gulf visit
stories.summary: PM meets Mohammed bin Salman in Saudi Arabia before further visits to regional allies, who may see him as more reliable than Trump Middle East crisis – live updates The UK has a “job” to help reopen the strait of Hormuz, Keir Starmer has said, as Iranian reports said the key shipping route was closed again just hours after a supposed ceasefire. stories.title: Anthropic keeps latest AI tool out of public’s hands for fear of enabling widespread hacking
stories.summary: AI company says purpose of its Claude Mythos model is to bolster defenses against hacking in common applications Anthropic on Tuesday said its yet-to-be-released artificial intelligence model called Claude Mythos has proven keenly adept at exposing software weaknesses. Mythos has laid bare thousands of vulnerabilities in commonly used applications for which no patch or fix exists, prompting the San Francisco-based AI startup to form an alliance with cybersecurity specialists to bolster defenses

stories.summary: ‘Largest coordinated strike’ against Hezbollah since war began prompts warnings Iran could pull out of ceasefire Middle East crisis – live updates Israel has carried out its largest attack on Lebanon since its war with Hezbollah began, killing at least 254 people and wounding 837, an assault that prompted Iranian officials to warn Tehran could withdraw from the ceasefire agreed with the US overnight. stories.summary: As Israel attacks on Beirut continue, Abbas Araghchi points to announcement that says ceasefire includes Lebanon while JD Vance says US never promised that Middle East ceasefire in serious doubt as Israel assaults Lebanon and Iran blocks oil tankers Will shipping in the strait of Hormuz – and oil prices – return to normal? stories.title: Europe fears getting stuck with the bill after Trump’s Iran deal
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Donald Trump makes a mess. Europe gets called on to clean it up, or at least pay a hefty price to deal with it. That’s a pattern the EU fears is about to repeat itself in the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. president announced a ceasefire agreement with Iran, five EU diplomats and officials said. European leaders had already promised to help clear the contested waterway once the fighting stopped. Not only could France, Germany and the U.K. now be on the hook to pay for an expens

stories.title: Trump sending Vance, Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is sending Vice President JD Vance and other top negotiators to Pakistan for mediated talks with Iran this weekend aimed at cementing a permanent ceasefire, the White House announced on Wednesday. Press secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed the U.S. participation in the talks, which will be held in Pakistan’s capital of Islamabad beginning on Saturday morning local time, during a press briefing, which came amid growing doubts that the two-week ceasefire announced on Tuesday

stories.title: Italy’s Giorgia Meloni attempts political reset after referendum defeat
stories.summary: ROME — Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni will try on Thursday to revamp her premiership, after rising energy prices and her ties to U.S. President Donald Trump helped contribute to a bruising referendum defeat last month. “The war created uncertainty, and a sense of fear has prevailed among those alarmed by the rise in petrol prices,” said Paolo Barelli, whip for the center-right Forza Italia party and member of the coalition government, after the electoral loss . During an address to parlia

stories.title: ‘I don’t see anyone accepting that’: Oil industry balks at Iran’s shipping toll
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s Iran peace plan is getting pushback from one key constituency: the oil industry. Oil company executives are reaching out to the White House, Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Vice President J.D. Vance to protest allowing Iran to charge tolls through the strategic Strait of Hormuz as a condition of peace talks, said one industry consultant granted anonymity to discuss relations with the administration. “Hell yes,” this person said when asked if executives were contacting

stories.title: Victims and bereaved families to get more time to challenge ‘unduly lenient’ sentences
stories.summary: David Lammy says those affected by a heinous crime cannot be expected to engage with the justice system within the existing 28-day limit Victims and bereaved families will be given six months to challenge “unduly lenient” sentences handed to criminals , under changes announced by David Lammy. Relatives of murder victims campaigned for the government to scrap the 28-day time limit to submit a formal request after an offender is sentenced. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Inclusion of Lebanon is significant difference in interpretation of truce agreed at 11th hour on Tuesday Middle East crisis – live updates The fate of the two-week ceasefire in the Iran conflict looked in peril on Wednesday as both sides gave divergent versions of what had been agreed, Israel intensified its bombing campaign in Lebanon and Iran halted the passage of oil tankers because of an alleged Israeli ceasefire breach. stories.title: US ignoring evidence Russia is helping Iran because it trusts Putin, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president tells podcast he has tried to draw White House’s attention to collaboration between Moscow and Tehran over strikes on US bases How the Ukraine and Iran wars are starting to overlap The US has ignored compelling evidence that Russia has been helping Iran to target US bases in the Middle East because it “trusts” Vladimir Putin, according to the Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy. stories.title: ‘Excessive’ financial risks threaten survival of many English universities, report warns
stories.summary: High levels of borrowing and rapid expansion among dangers identified by Higher Education Policy Institute Many English universities are taking excessive financial risks that threaten not only their own survival but that of others in the sector, a thinktank has warned. High levels of borrowing at some institutions and rapid expansion of student numbers are among the dangers identified in a report by the Higher Education Policy Institute (Hepi). Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

stories.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody
stories.summary: Court reporter Alican Uludag has been in pre-trial detention in Istanbul for six weeks, charged with "insulting the president." His lawyers say this is completely disproportionate.

stories.title: Ceasefire offers Trump exit as Iran war becomes political liability
stories.summary: The ceasefire deal with Iran might come at the right moment for Donald Trump, as it could stop the erosion of domestic support for the President. But Trump is left facing scrutiny over why and for what did the US get into war.

stories.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
stories.summary: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.

stories.title: Gambia appoints British barrister to prosecute gruesome Jammeh-era crimes
stories.summary: Ex-President Yahya Jammeh's 22-year rule was known for enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings.

stories.title: Iran's defence-industrial base 'completely' destroyed, US defence secretary says
stories.summary: The Pentagon chief also said the United States is "watching" Iran's enriched uranium, which Washington targeted in strikes last year, and will take action if Tehran does not give it up.

stories.title: Cuts to NDIS to be focus of Labor’s quietly launched razor gang ahead of May budget
stories.summary: Exclusive: Taskforce led by former Treasury official Anthea Long will advise on cost-cutting options for $52bn program Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor has quietly established a razor gang to drive budget savings in the national disability insurance scheme, as it works to further rein in costs ahead of next month’s federal budget. An NDIS Sustainability Taskforce was established within the health department

stories.title: ‘Non-survivable’: heatwaves are already breaching human limits, with worse to come, study finds
stories.summary: Analysis of six extreme heatwaves found when temperature and humidity were accounted for, all were potentially deadly for older people Extreme heat is already creating “non-survivable” conditions for humans in heatwaves that have killed thousands and likely many more, according to new research that warns people are more susceptible to rising temperatures than first thought. Scientists re-examined six extreme heatwaves between 2003 and 2024 and found that when temperature, humidity and the body’s

stories.title: Greece announces social media ban for under-15s, citing anxiety and sleep problems
stories.summary: PM says ban will come into force in January if it is backed by parliament and calls for united action across EU Greece has announced a social media ban for under-15s from 1 January, with the country’s prime minister, Kyriakos Mitsotakis, citing rising anxiety, sleep problems and the addictive design of online platforms – although he acknowledged it may incur the wrath of some children. “We have decided to go ahead with a difficult but necessary measure: ban access to social media for children un

stories.title: Man who caused gas blast that destroyed partner’s house jailed for 11 years
stories.summary: Paul Solway ignited explosion that damaged total of six terrace houses in Derby after his partner had kicked him out A man who blew up a terrace house by causing gas to leak from a pipe and setting fire to a chair after his partner kicked him out has been jailed for 11 years. Paul Solway was having a “meltdown” when he caused the explosion at his partner Joanne Waterfall’s home in Alvaston in Derby on the evening of 10 June last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ministers unveil ‘right to try’ plan to help disabled people find work
stories.summary: Threat of losing benefits will be lifted but campaigners say more help needed to tackle hostile workplaces The government has unveiled its plan to allow disabled people to try work without fear of losing their benefits, but campaigners warn the policy does not go far enough to tackle hostile workplaces. Legislation laid before parliament on Thursday will mean that people who start work or volunteering no longer automatically face a benefit reassessment, a prospect disabled people said was holdin

stories.title: LA teen loses eye after being shot by US agent at No Kings march, lawyer says
stories.summary: USC student Tucker Collins’s attorney accuses homeland security of ‘overt act of repression’ at Los Angeles protest A freshman at the University of Southern California has lost an eye after he was shot last month with a “less-lethal” projectile by a Department of Homeland Security agent at a No Kings march, according to his attorney. On 28 March, Tucker Collins, 18, took to the streets of downtown Los Angeles to photograph throngs of protesters, who held signs and chanted slogans denouncing the

stories.title: Newly created Polymarket accounts win big on well-timed Iran ceasefire bets
stories.summary: Customers make hundreds of thousands of dollars as records show substantial bets made before announcement A group of new accounts on the prediction market Polymarket made highly specific, well-timed bets on whether the US and Iran would reach a ceasefire on Tuesday, resulting in hundreds of thousands of dollars in profits for these new customers. These bets were made even though, in the hours before a two-week ceasefire was announced on Tuesday, Donald Trump’s rhetoric had escalated sharply and

stories.title: ‘Deeply concerned’ Australia says Lebanon should be included in Middle East ceasefire
stories.summary: Joint statement calling for aid worker protections also signed by Brazil, Colombia, Indonesia, Jordan, Sierra Leone and the UK Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia says Lebanon must be included in the Middle East ceasefire and has led a group of other countries in expressing deep concern about “the worsening humanitarian situation and displacement crisis in Lebanon”. stories.title: Trump and former loyalist Marjorie Taylor Greene trade jabs as Maga split over Iran widens – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Success or surrender? Iran ceasefire exposes rift in Trump’s Maga movement Sign up for the Breaking News US email Pete Hegseth repeated Donald Trump’s social media comments that Iran will cease uranium enrichment – a condition that Tehran has previously refused to budge on. “Any material they should not have, will be removed right now,” Hegseth said. “The president has been clear from the beginning, there will be no Iranian nuclear weapons.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife found guilty of attempted manslaughter
stories.summary: Prosecutors alleged Gerhardt Konig, 47, had planned to kill Arielle Konig during a birthday trip to Honolulu A Hawaii anesthesiologist who was accused of trying to murder his wife on a cliffside hike last year has been convicted of attempted manslaughter, a lesser charge. A Honolulu jury returned the verdict against Gerhardt Konig, 47, on Wednesday after a day of deliberations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germans pining for Gerhard Schröder forget his errors and ties to Putin. The SPD needs a fresh approach | Katja Hoyer
stories.summary: The former chancellor was the chief architect of Germany’s dependence on Russian fossil fuels and cuts to defence spending. Both haunt the country today The former German chancellor, Gerhard Schröder, is enjoying a curious political revival. Not so long ago, his reputation seemed in tatters. In light of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many came to regard his longstanding ties to Russia and personal friendship with Vladimir Putin as self-serving. Fellow Social Democrats (SPD) tried to expel h

stories.title: ‘A step back from the brink’: European leaders welcome US-Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Announcement of deal met with relief and calls for strait of Hormuz to be reopened and permanent end to hostilities US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire Middle East crisis – live updates European leaders have welcomed the US-Iran ceasefire deal while calling for the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and a permanent end to hostilities, including in Lebanon. The US and Iran agreed a two-week conditional ceasefire on Tuesday, including a temporary reopening of the strait of Hormuz, after last

stories.title: Iran doubles down on executions amid US-Israeli war
stories.summary: Iran has executed 14 individuals in recent weeks, with the hangings continuing unabated regardless of the US-Israeli military campaign against the country.

stories.title: Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say
stories.summary: Authorities in Nigeria's restive north put the death toll at three, but residents and humanitarians point to a higher toll. The attack occurred in an area where kidnapping gangs and Islamist militants are active.

stories.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
stories.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

stories.title: Germany news: Lufthansa cabin crew to strike on Friday, UFO trade union says
stories.summary: The latest strike to hit Germany's flagship carrier hails from the cabin crew union UFO. Meanwhile, Berlin is walking back a contentious plan about young men seeking permission to go abroad for long periods.

stories.title: Iran war: who gained ground, who lost influence
stories.summary: While civilians in the region pay the highest price, the Iran war has reshaped power balances far beyond the Gulf. Some states have gained leverage. Others have lost influence.

stories.summary: Voters across two states and one union territory in India will cast their ballot in Assembly elections tomorrow.

stories.title: Nigeria begins mass trial of 500 terrorism suspects

stories.title: BTS turned millions on to K-pop. But now it's caught between Korea and the world

stories.summary: Participants in Amsterdam and Utrecht join “offline club” events, handing in their phones to spend time on creative activities and in-person interaction.

stories.title: Camel calf Sophia born at Whipsnade Zoo, boosting conservation focus

stories.title: Watch: Fire damages roof of Rio's Olympic velodrome
stories.summary: Around 80 firefighters and 20 fire trucks tackled the blaze, the state's military fire department said, adding that no one was hurt.

stories.title: Reports of Iran closing Strait of Hormuz 'completely unacceptable,' White House says
stories.summary: One of the major points in the two-week ceasefire agreement was that Iran would allow the resumption of shipping through Hormuz, easing a disruption in the flow of oil and gas which has sent prices soaring around the world.

stories.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
stories.summary: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Turkey puts 11 leaders of LGBTQ+ rights association on trial for 'obscenity'
stories.summary: Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but the LGBTQ+ community is frequently targeted by authorities who blame it for the country's declining birth rate.

stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
stories.summary: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to

The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported t

stories.title: Iran still refuses to reopen Strait of Hormuz as Trump threatens infrastructure strikes

Israel also agreed to the ceasefire,

stories.title: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn
stories.summary: Category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds at centre in excess of 150km/h Tropical Cyclone Vaianu forming in the Pacific could bring life-threatening winds and heavy rain to New Zealand later this week, forecasters have said, with strong wind watches issued for the entire North Island. The category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds around the centre in excess of 150km/h, MetService said on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe cannot bet on a post-Trump US turning back to sanity | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: If the president’s first term didn’t inoculate the American body politic against tyranny, there is no guarantee that a second dose will work Donald Trump is a despot and the US is a democracy. These things can be true simultaneously but not indefinitely. There is now deadlock in the struggle between a president who would be king and a constitution drafted in repudiation of monarchy. But it is a battle to the death. Tyranny will either break the spirit of the republic or be quelled by it. Since t

stories.title: Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam
stories.summary: Orla Wates, 19, who died after incident on popular Ha Giang loop, described as ‘beautiful, independent and very funny’ The family of a British teenager have paid tribute to their daughter who died after a motorcycle crash on a popular route in Vietnam. The incident occurred on the Ha Giang loop in the country’s north, and Orla Wates, 19, died at the Viet Duc university hospital in Hanoi, according to Viet Nam News. Continue reading...

stories.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
stories.summary: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventua

stories.title: North Korea fires ballistic missiles toward sea
stories.summary: North Korea has fired more missiles toward the sea for a second day. The back-to-back launches came as Pyongyang reiterated that it has no intentions of mending ties with Seoul.

stories.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

stories.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?

stories.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?

stories.title: India's digital census prompts fear of hidden agendas

stories.title: How Pakistan brokered a US-Iran ceasefire, and what's next

stories.title: Thousands of Indigenous people march in Brasilia for land rights

stories.summary: Taiwan’s opposition leader makes historic six-day trip to Beijing to “boost dialogue and promote peace” ahead of a heavily anticipated summit between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

stories.summary: Major shipping lines express cautious optimism over transit opportunities in the Strait of Hormuz after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced, but stress the urgent need for full clarity before any vessels move.

stories.summary: The director of the Thai zoo where Moo Deng lives said security would be upped after the incident.

stories.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
stories.summary: It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

stories.title: Israel’s political forces are not happy with US-Iran ceasefire deal - here is why
stories.summary: In a rare moment of consensus, Israel’s political forces agree in their criticism of the US-Iran two-week ceasefire. And while the centre-left politicians are unhappy with PM Netanyahu, the conservatives are criticising Trump.

stories.title: Syrian accused of wartime torture for al-Assad denies charges in Netherlands court
stories.summary: The trial is based on universal jurisdiction, a legal principle that allows suspects to be prosecuted for international offenses such as war crimes even if they are committed in another country.

stories.title: Lebanon 'separate skirmish' and not part of Iran ceasefire deal, Trump tells US media
stories.summary: Israel has rarely struck central Beirut since the outbreak of the latest Israel-Hezbollah war on 2 March but has regularly struck southern and eastern Lebanon and Beirut's southern suburbs.

stories.title: Tíz éve még hemzsegtek a békák Farmoson, idén kevesebb mint százat mentettek meg

stories.title: Bajnokok Ligája negyeddöntő: PSG–Liverpool 2-0

stories.title: Ez a játék tényleg egy maraton
stories.summary: Nem való mindenkinek, sokan nem tudják, mi értelme, de aki nyomja, az imádja. A Bungie a Marathonnal egy olyan lövöldözős játékot csinált, ami szinte minden elemében lenyűgöző, de olyan könyörtelen, hogy kevesen fogják meglátni minden szépségét.

stories.title: A Long Island-i sorozatgyilkos bevallotta, nyolc embert is megölt
stories.summary: Rex Heuermann 1993 és 2010 között gyilkolt nőket New York közelében. Hét gyilkossággal vádolták, egészen szerdáig ártatlannak vallotta magát.

stories.title: Az egész EU-ban elsődlegessé tennék a digitális forgalmi engedélyt, de nem kötelezővé
stories.summary: Az EP szakbizottsága az adatok megosztásával igyekezne megakadályozni az uniós tagállamok közti csalásokat az autók kilométerórájának tekergetésével.

stories.title: Ne árulhassanak az EU-ban vega bacont vagy steaket ilyen néven, szavazta meg az EP szaktestülete
stories.summary: Az Európai Parlament mezőgazdasági bizottsága jóváhagyta a kormányokkal kötött alkut, amellyel korlátoznák a nem állati eredetű termékek megnevezését a boltok polcain.

stories.title: Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia and Russian-occupied regions killed at least five, including a 12-year-old boy. Ukraine has been pushing for a ceasefire during the Orthodox Easter, which is later this week.

stories.title: Trump says 'whole civilisation will die tonight' if Iran ultimatum expires
stories.summary: Previously, the US president has warned that unless Tehran reopened the Strait of Hormuz, the US would unleash what he called the "complete demolition" of Iran's critical infrastructure overnight on Wednesday.

stories.title: France's Sarkozy maintains innocence at Libya election funding trial
stories.summary: Sarkozy was sentenced to five years in prison for his alleged part in a scheme to obtain funds from the government of Libya's leader Muammar Gaddafi in exchange for political and diplomatic favours.

stories.title: 'Only the president knows' what he will do on Iran, White House says in statement
stories.summary: Trump had threatened to target Iranian infrastructure unless Tehran agreed a deal to end the war on Tuesday, saying he was "considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil."

stories.title: Anti-war protesters rally outside US embassy office in Tel Aviv
stories.summary: A small group of anti-war protesters gathered outside the US embassy’s Tel Aviv office, calling for an end to the conflict before police moved to disperse the demonstration.

stories.title: Police destroy opium poppy fields in Pakistan
stories.summary: Pakistani police destroyed hectares of opium poppy fields in a tribal region near the Afghan border, as farmers said poverty leaves them few alternatives.

stories.title: Bangladesh launches measles vaccination drive as child death toll passes 100
stories.summary: UN assists in emergency vaccination drive as country battles worst surge in cases in years amid fall in vaccination rates Bangladesh is battling its worse measles outbreak in years, with more than 100 children dead amid a rise in unvaccinated infants. The government, in partnership with the United Nations, has begun conducting an emergency measles-rubella vaccination drive for children across the country, after more than 900 cases were confirmed since March. Continue reading...

stories.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
stories.summary: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime. Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at

stories.title: Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas
stories.summary: Politicians warn party’s pledge to ‘punish’ countries seeking justice for slavery will harm and isolate Britain Commonwealth politicians say they will not back down from seeking reparations as UK public figures, including a former Reform insider, warn the rightwing party’s pledge to “punish” countries seeking justice for slavery would harm and isolate Britain. This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power

stories.title: World held hostage by reliance on fossil fuels, Christiana Figueres warns – and climate health impacts are ‘mother of all injustices’
stories.summary: Exclusive: Former UN climate chief to co-chair Lancet Commission examining how sea-level rise is reshaping health, wellbeing and inequality What are the health impacts of sea-level rise, and who should pay? Christiana Figueres: Sea-level rise is a health crisis and we must hold polluters accountable Countries are being “held hostage” by their reliance on fossil fuels, a former UN climate chief has warned, describing the health impacts of climate change as “the mother of all injustices”. Christia

stories.title: New Zealand asks US to send fuel tankers to Pacific to alleviate pressure caused by Iran war
stories.summary: After meeting with Marco Rubio, foreign minister Winston Peters says he made sure US understands ‘significant economic impacts on New Zealand and Pacific’ Middle East crisis – live updates New Zealand has called on the US to send fuel tankers to the Pacific to help alleviate some of the significant economic and fuel pressure caused by the war in the Middle East. Winston Peters, New Zealand’s foreign minister, met the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, in Washington on Tuesday, where they discus

stories.title: Úgy buzdít választásra Wáberer György, hogy maradtak az államhoz kötődő pozíciói
stories.summary: Korábban miniszterelnöki megbízott és kormánybiztos is volt az ország egyik leggazdagabb embere, most úgy buzdít az Oroszország és Európa közti választásra, hogy például még a MÁV igazgatósági tagja.

stories.title: Pollster estimates strong two-thirds majority for Tisza
stories.summary: According to Medián's survey, the Tisza Party could win between 138 and 143 seats in Parliament, while Fidesz can expect to win 49 to 55 seats.

stories.title: Jogerősen is elítélték a pécsi Volvo-botrány vádlottjait, egyiküket bilincsben vitték el a tárgyalóteremből
stories.summary: Gazdasági csalásért és pénzmosásért jogerősen bűnösnek mondta ki a Pécsi Ítélőtábla a másod- és negyedrendű vádlottakat.

stories.title: A bíróság szerint alkalmatlan volt RFK Jr. jelöltje, ezért a miniszter átírta a vakcinabizottság alkalmassági elvárását
stories.summary: A bíró szerint 15 jelöltből csak hatnak volt releváns tapasztalata.

stories.title: „Nem megyünk sehova” – mondta az amerikai védelmi miniszter, aki szerint Iránnak ki kéne küldenie a postagalambjait
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth arról beszélt a Pentagon sajtótájékoztatóján, hogy a tűzszünet ellenére készenlétben marad az amerikai hadsereg Irán körül.

stories.title: T. Danny: Összeesett az orrom pár nap leforgása alatt
stories.summary: Közhelyes marad, de itt-ott betalál: T. Danny dokusorozatának zárása már nem kerüli ki a lényeget, a tagadást és a visszafordíthatatlan következményeket.

stories.title: A Telex történetének eddigi legnagyobb választási műsorával készülünk április 12-re
stories.summary: Részvételi adatok, helyszíni bejelentkezések, élő elemzések, térkép és egy exkluzív kutatási adat, amelyet urnazáráskor fogunk publikálni. Érdemes lesz nálunk követni a választás napját!

stories.title: A legjobbak azok a járványok, amik soha nem történnek meg
stories.summary: A felújított pécsi Nemzeti Virológiai Laboratóriumban jártunk, megnéztük, hol kutatják a tudósok a legveszélyesebb vírusokat. A labor munkája nem látványos, mégis létfontosságú: itt készülnek fel a járványokra, amelyek talán soha nem következnek be.

stories.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
stories.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

stories.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
stories.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

stories.title: How to insulate your household from soaring energy bills
stories.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, record-breaking heat waves and blackouts, producing electricity at home looks more appealing than ever.

stories.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan held 'useful' peace talks, Kabul says
stories.summary: Clashes between the Afghan Taliban and Pakistan reignited in February. Pakistan accuses Afghanistan of harboring hostile militants, which Kabul denies. China has offered to mediate in easing tensions.

stories.title: US says Lebanon not part of ceasefire deal, Iran says it is
stories.summary: Donald Trump has said Lebanon is not part of the two-week ceasefire deal that was agreed overnight. Israel launched a massive wave of strikes on Lebanon after the deal was announced. Follow DW.

stories.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
stories.summary: Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

stories.title: Centauros: Greece's anti-drone system that changes 'war of drones' at low cost
stories.summary: The anti-drone system, named after a mythical creature symbolising untamed forces of nature, uses electronic warfare technology to detect and neutralise UAVs without munitions.

stories.title: Iran war and energy supply disruptions accelerate South Caucasus collaboration
stories.summary: When it comes to connecting the continents of Asia and Europe, Azerbaijan and Georgia play a vital role in the transit of energy. The leaders of both countries have outlined how they will work together to broaden the Middle Corridor.

stories.title: Three gunmen 'neutralised' in shooting incident near Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: Authorities said a comprehensive investigation is being carried out with law enforcement units under the coordination of the chief public prosecutor's office.

stories.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
stories.summary: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".

stories.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
stories.summary: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.

stories.title: Trump's Iran ultimatum: When does it expire and how serious is his threat to 'blow up everything'?
stories.summary: US president warns a civilisation could be about to disappear if the regime in Tehran does not respond to his ultimatum after six weeks of war by Tuesday night. What are the possible scenarios? Euronews explains:

stories.title: Has Artemis II shown we can land on the Moon again?
stories.summary: The Artemis II mission has been near flawless to date, but has the test flight shown Nasa is ready to send humans to the lunar surface?
